description: A cognitive aging laboratory has collected Digit Symbol Substitution Test (DSST) data from 30 older adults at ~/pebl/data/dsst_data.csv (columns: participant_id, trial, elapsed_time_ms, rt_ms, correct). Generate a report at ~/pebl/analysis/dsst_report.json based on trials within a 90-second window (elapsed_time_ms <= 90000). The report must include the Clinical Score-90 (total correct trials), Accuracy Rate (correct/total), and Incidental Learning Index (ILI) for each participant. The ILI is the difference between the mean rt_ms of the first 15 correct trials and the mean rt_ms of the last 15 correct trials, set to null if fewer than 30 correct trials exist. Participants with an Accuracy Rate below 0.20 are to be flagged as excluded. The final JSON must include a 'participants' list with 'id', 'clinical_score_90s', 'accuracy_rate', and 'incidental_learning_ms' (or 'excluded': true with 'reason'), along with 'group_mean_score' and 'group_mean_learning_ms' for all valid participants, ignoring null ILI values.
